answer:
**Several works in the Falsafa corpus address the figures of the Sufi or the mystic, primarily through poetic references in Urdu ghazals and nazms that portray them as spiritual seekers, ascetics, or bearers of esoteric knowledge. These appear in collections by Allama Iqbal, Mirza Ghalib, and Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq. No dedicated prose treatises on Sufism or mysticism are evident in the results, though the poetry engages these archetypes extensively via imagery of Sufi lore, dervishes (darwesh/faqir), and ecstatic states.[^1][^2][^3][^4][^5]**

### Key Works and Contexts:
- **Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1***: Poems reference Sufis and mystics as embodiments of spiritual insight or hypocrisy critiqued through asceticism (zuhd) vs. ecstasy (rindi). E.g., a cleric with "Sufi air"; mystics as solitary souls.[^1][^6]
- **Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2***: Sufis appear in visions of divine unity found "in the heart's chamber of darkness."[^2]
- **Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3***: Dervishes as humble warriors of passion and reason.[^7]
- **Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib***: Frequent motifs of mystics' company (requiring caution), their visions, and dervish-like wandering or wine-surrendered states.[^3][^8][^9]
- **Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq***: Sufi texts as paths to heart-purification; true dervish (darwesh) vs. comfort-seeking faqir.[^4][^10][^11]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44 ("Zuhd aur Rindi"), [p-52b34d](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44/translation/#p-52b34d).
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 20 ("Salimi"), [p-e4b1ba](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/20/translation/#p-e4b1ba).
[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 22 ("Chahiye achhon ko jitna chahiye"), [p-c886a3](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/22/translation/#p-c886a3).
[^4]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 49 ("Voh kaun hai jo mujh pe ta'assuf nahin karta"), [p-655b56](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/49/translation/#p-655b56).
[^5]: Corpus search for "Sufi" and "mystic" (English translations) yields ~18 hits, all in these works.
[^6]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44, [p-8c3c35](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44/translation/#p-8c3c35).
[^7]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 4 ("Dil se jo baat nikalti hai asar rakhti hai"), [p-819a53](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/04/translation/#p-819a53).
[^8]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 36 ("Dil-e-naadaan tujhe hua kya hai"), [p-70f710](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/36/translation/#p-70f710).
[^9]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 158 ("Paye-nazr-e-karam tohfa hai sharm-na-rasai ka"), [p-280a13](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/158/translation/#p-280a13).
[^10]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 25 ("Kab haq-parast zahid-e-jannat-parast hai"), [p-e44bc0](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/25/translation/#p-e44bc0).
[^11]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 25 (transliteration snippet confirming "faqir").
